name: Alpha Modus Holdings, Inc.

profile.description: Alpha Modus Holdings is a vertical AI micro-cap focused on real-time in-store shopper engagement and attribution using a patented 'closed-loop' retail AI framework (Sense -> Decide -> Deliver -> Attribute). Subsidiaries include Alpha Modus Financial Services, which is deploying the AlphaCash network of financial service kiosks. Founded in 2014 and headquartered in Cornelius, NC.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Alpha Modus went public via SPAC and has not yet generated meaningful product revenue; AlphaCash Q1 2026 pilot with ~100 kiosks across a Texas retailer is early-stage, with management citing only a $2,000 monthly revenue baseline per kiosk [1]. AI Deployment (1/2): Proprietary 'closed-loop' retail AI framework exists and has AlphaCash pilot shipping to a national retailer, but no quantified revenue or customer ROI metrics disclosed [1]. Market Share (0/2): A ~$18M market cap micro-cap competing against cashierless/retail-AI giants (Amazon Just Walk Out, Sensormatic, AiFi); no meaningful share in any defined category [2]. Pipeline (1/2): AlphaCash rollout targeting 4,000+ U.S. retail locations contingent on pilot results, plus a stated IP enforcement pipeline of 23 cases [2][3]. R&D Intensity (0/1): Tiny team (~15 employees); more spend is going to patent litigation than forward R&D [3].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): Company mission reads as generic AI-retail vendor marketing ('merge artificial intelligence, retail media, and financial access'), interchangeable with many retail-tech peers [2]. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of meaningful community or charitable programs given the micro-cap scale [2]. Employee Retention (0/2): ~15 employees with a revolving cast of newly hired executives (Gallagher as CRO, Vij as VP Technology) indicates organizational build-out rather than retention data [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO William Alessi is founder and seed investor, and executed a patent monetization agreement via Alpha Modus Ventures (an entity he controls) that provides the company an option to acquire — a structure that can align or conflict, but reflects personal capital commitment [3].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: HARD RULE FAILURE — LIQUIDITY FLOOR: Stock price of $0.36 x 818,751 avg daily volume = $295k daily dollar liquidity, well below the $1M IOWN minimum. Infrastructure score is forced to 0 regardless of other metrics [4]. The rest of the picture confirms the rule: Q3 2025 EPS of -$0.10, TTM net loss of ~$4.3M, no revenue visibility beyond the pilot, a $250M S-3 shelf filing indicating material dilution risk, and ~$18M market cap [1][3]. No dividend; no earnings; reliance on patent settlement proceeds for cash flow [3].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (4/9): Alpha Modus IS an AI company, which provides natural resilience to AI substitution risk for its core product. However, its patent-enforcement business model (23 active IP cases, lawsuits against Lowe's, Broadcom, Sensormatic/Johnson Controls, A2Z, Navori, meldCX) is inherently defensive and depends on courts affirming the IP against well-resourced defendants [3]. Retail AI is a crowded space where Amazon, Google, and well-funded startups have substantial technology advantages. AI Opportunity (1/1): Vertical AI retail platform is shipping (AlphaCash pilot) with stated expansion to 4,000+ locations; any patent-licensing revenue is also AI-derived, meeting the 'meaningful AI revenue' tiebreaker threshold [1][3].

infinite_game.summary: Alpha Modus exhibits finite-game characteristics dominated by patent-enforcement litigation rather than product-led expansion. The strategy — sue retail incumbents for IP infringement, monetize settlements, fund additional litigation — is inherently adversarial and hasn't been validated by durable product revenue. AlphaCash pilot is an operational positive but is nascent.

just_cause.analysis: Stated mission around bridging digital and physical retail with AI is generic; the company's actual operating posture (patent litigation against retail giants) doesn't read as a just cause in Sinek's sense but as a legal monetization strategy [2][3].

trusting_teams.analysis: Small founder-led team with recent senior hires (CRO, VP Technology) suggests organizational build rather than entrenched culture dysfunction; no public data on retention or culture red flags given scale [2].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Management frames competitors (Lowe's, Broadcom, Sensormatic) as targets for IP enforcement rather than worthy rivals to learn from — a classic finite-game posture of trying to 'win' through legal leverage rather than product competition [3].

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from IP holding to operational AI company (AlphaCash kiosk rollout) shows willingness to evolve the business model; execution on scaling the pilot will determine whether this is genuine flexibility or a side bet [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Reliance on patent-litigation settlements, a $250M S-3 shelf that signals likely dilution, and funding agreements through founder-controlled entities raise governance concerns rather than demonstrate courageous long-term leadership [3].

investment_thesis: Alpha Modus is a ~$18M micro-cap vertical AI company pursuing a two-track strategy: patent enforcement against retail technology users (Lowe's, Broadcom, Sensormatic/Johnson Controls, Navori, A2Z, meldCX) and commercial deployment of the AlphaCash financial service kiosk network [1][3]. The Q1 2026 pilot with ~100 AlphaCash kiosks across a Texas retail chain, and an identified opportunity of 4,000+ additional U.S. retail locations, offers operational upside if the pilot converts at management's conservative $2,000/month per-kiosk baseline [1]. Recent IP enforcement settlements (Mood Media dismissed with prejudice January 2026; additional settlements announced March 2026) provide episodic cash flows [3]. One analyst 12-month price target of $1.50 implies material upside from $0.36.
thesis_continued: The bear case is decisive. Alpha Modus FAILS the IOWN liquidity floor: $0.36 price x 818k avg daily volume = $295k of daily dollar liquidity, well below the $1M threshold, which forces Infrastructure to 0 [4]. TTM losses of ~$4.3M and no product revenue visibility beyond early-stage AlphaCash pilots mean the company depends on patent settlements and dilutive capital raises (a $250M S-3 shelf is filed) to fund operations [3]. The patent-enforcement business model is inherently adversarial, and governance concerns exist around funding agreements with CEO-controlled entities (Alpha Modus Ventures) [3]. Retail AI is a crowded space where Amazon, Google, and better-funded startups own meaningful technology. The combination of micro-cap illiquidity, going-concern-level losses, litigation-dependent cash flow, and dilution risk makes AMOD unsuitable for IOWN. SELL.
key_catalysts: AlphaCash Q1 2026 pilot converting to 4,000+ location rollout at $2,000/month per kiosk, producing material recurring revenue [1]
Patent enforcement settlements generating episodic cash flow — Mood Media resolved January 2026, additional settlements announced March 2026, 23 cases pipeline [3]
IP monetization agreement for Broadcom Fibre Channel patents producing settlement or licensing revenue [3]
key_risks: Liquidity floor failure ($295k daily dollar liquidity vs. $1M IOWN minimum) makes the position untradeable for institutional portfolios [4]
$250M S-3 shelf filing implies material equity dilution risk from future capital raises [3]
Patent litigation outcomes against well-resourced defendants (Lowe's, Broadcom) are uncertain, and adverse rulings would eliminate a primary revenue pathway [3]
sources: Alpha Modus Holdings AlphaCash Q1 2026 pilot press release (100 Texas kiosks, 4,000 location target), StockTitan and MarketBeat, March 2026
Alpha Modus company website, Simply Wall St and Stock Titan leadership profiles (CEO William Alessi, CRO Gallagher, VP Tech Vij), 2025-2026
Alpha Modus patent infringement lawsuits (Lowe's, Broadcom, Sensormatic/Johnson Controls, A2Z, Navori, meldCX); Mood Media settlement January 2026; IP monetization agreement with Alpha Modus Ventures
